name: Martín River Trail (GR 262)
name_original: Montalbán - Peñas Royas por el Sendero del Río Martín (GR 262)
description: Jogging the difficult 8.8-mile Montalban - Penas Royas via the Martin River Trail (GR 262) offers a challenging run in Aragon, Spain.
region_intro: Jogging around Montalbán offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region features varied terrain, including river trails and loops that traverse through natural areas. Elevation changes are present on many routes, with some trails gaining significant altitude. The area provides opportunities for running through different natural environments, from river valleys to more elevated sections. Best jogging routes around Montalbán The most popular jogging route is Martín River Trail (GR 262), a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the course of the Martín River, offering a challenging run. Another top favourite among local runners is Alcaine loop from Alcaine, a difficult 9.5 miles (15.3 km) path. This route forms a loop around Alcaine, featuring varied terrain and elevation changes. Local runners also love the Peña del Rebollar loop from Manuel Beltrán (antiguo Hospital), a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Jogging around Montalbán is defined by river valleys, varied elevation, and natural loops. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult running routes. The routes in Montalbán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 39 runners have used komoot to explore Montalbán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Montalbán?
A: There are 5 dedicated running routes around Montalbán listed on komoot. These routes offer a range of experiences, from moderate to difficult, traversing varied terrain including river valleys and elevated sections.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate running trails for beginners in Montalbán?
A: While many routes in Montalbán are classified as difficult due to elevation changes, there is one moderate option: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Jarque de la Val&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 6.7 km long with a more manageable elevation gain, making it suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Montalbán?
A: The jogging routes around Montalbán feature a diverse landscape. You can expect varied terrain, including scenic river trails and loops that wind through natural areas. Many routes also include significant elevation changes, offering a challenging experience for runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Montalbán area?
A: Yes, several popular running routes in Montalbán are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaine loop from Alcaine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38145915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Rebollar loop from Manuel Beltrán (antiguo Hospital)&lt;/a&gt; both offer loop experiences, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks I can see while running near Montalbán?
A: The region around Montalbán offers several points of interest. While running, you might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5377977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Santa Engracia&lt;/a&gt;, or pass through charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estercuel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3694497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroyas&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often showcase the natural beauty of the area.

Q: Are the running trails around Montalbán suitable for families?
A: Given the prevalence of difficult routes with significant elevation changes, many of the longer trails around Montalbán might be challenging for younger children or families seeking very easy, flat paths. However, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Jarque de la Val&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that could be more suitable for active families looking for a shorter, less strenuous run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Montalbán?
A: The running routes in Montalbán are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Runners often praise the varied terrain and the natural landscapes that define the area's trails.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Montalbán?
A: Generally, most natural trails in Spain are dog-friendly, provided your dog is kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions in Montalbán, particularly within protected natural areas.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Montalbán?
A: The longest running route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Santa Engracia – Montalbán loop from Montalbán&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 21.2 km (13.2 miles) and involves substantial elevation gain, making it a challenging and rewarding long-distance run.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow a river in Montalbán?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983331372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Martín River Trail (GR 262)&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent route that follows the course of the Martín River. This 14.2 km trail offers a challenging run alongside the river, showcasing the natural beauty of the river valley.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Montalbán?
A: Montalbán experiences a continental climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winters can be cold, and higher elevation routes might experience frost or occasional snow.
tips: The town of Peñas Royas is located within the municipality of Montalbán and on the banks of the Martín River. It is surrounded by a very picturesque landscape as well as singular due to the curious geological formations of the area. The path along the river is also very picturesque and does not present technical difficulties, ideal for a recreational walk.

faqs.question: Where is the Montalban - Penas Royas via the Martin River Trail (GR 262) located?
faqs.answer: This trail is located in the Comunidad de Teruel, Spain, specifically within the Martín River Cultural Park. The 'Montalban' in the trail name refers to Montalbán, a municipality in Aragon, Spain, not the Montalban in the Philippines.

faqs.question: What is the Martín River Cultural Park and are there any entrance fees or regulations?
faqs.answer: The Martín River Cultural Park is a protected area in Aragon, Spain, known for its natural beauty, archaeological sites, and cultural heritage. While there are generally no entrance fees for the park itself, specific attractions or facilities within the park might have their own charges. Always check local signage or the park's official website for the most current regulations regarding activities like hiking, camping, or specific access points.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available in the towns along the GR 262, such as Montalbán or Peñas Royas. Look for designated parking areas near the trailheads. Specific parking details can vary, so it's advisable to check local maps or tourism information for the most convenient spots.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead by public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to access the GR 262 in the Martín River Cultural Park may be limited, especially to specific trailheads. Buses might serve larger towns like Montalbán. It's recommended to research local bus schedules for routes connecting to the start or end points of your planned section of the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Montalban - Penas Royas via the Martin River Trail (GR 262) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This specific route is rated as 'difficult' for jogging, with a significant elevation gain of approximately 388 meters over 14.2 kilometers. While sections of the broader GR 262 are of medium difficulty, this particular segment is more challenging and may not be ideal for absolute beginners in jogging or trail running.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Martin River Trail (GR 262)?
faqs.answer: The GR 262 generally features varied terrain typical of a long-distance trail in Spain, including dirt paths, rocky sections, and possibly some paved or gravel roads through villages. Given its 'difficult' rating for jogging, expect some challenging ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers scenic views of the Martín River valley and the surrounding landscape of the Cultural Park. A notable highlight along this route is the settlement of Peñas Royas. The broader Martín River Cultural Park is also known for its archaeological sites and natural formations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route extensively follows the Sendero del Río Martín (100% of the route) and also passes through sections of the Vuelta al Barranco Ancho (64%), Ruta de la Muela de Montalbán (4%), and PR-TE 118 - Sendero del Barranco de la Cingla (1%).

faqs.question: Is the Montalban - Penas Royas via the Martin River Trail (GR 262) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within natural parks in Spain, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet. Check for any specific local restrictions within the Martín River Cultural Park before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Martin River Trail (GR 262)?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, making jogging more challenging. Winter can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, towns like Montalbán and Peñas Royas, which are along or near the GR 262, offer amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options. These can be good places to refuel or stay overnight if you're planning a multi-day trip on the GR 262.
